pattern: A string is a repetition of a block exactly when it reappears inside its own doubling with the first and last characters trimmed, because a non-trivial rotation only realigns the string when its period divides its length.
hints: If the string is some block repeated, the block length must divide the total length, which limits the candidate periods you ever need to test.
Doubling the string and searching for the original inside it (ignoring the trivial match at the very start) reveals whether the string equals a shifted copy of itself.
Build s + s, strip one character off each end, and check whether the original s still occurs somewhere in that trimmed concatenation.

edge_inputs.description: almost periodic but with a trailing remainder
a longer block repeated several times
single character cannot be at least two copies
two identical characters do form a repetition
a near-period that does not divide the length fails
repeated block where the block itself looks periodic
prefix repeats but the whole string does not

stress.description: deliberately periodic strings built from a random block repeated a random number of times
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    block_len = rng.randint(1, 8)
    block = ''.join(rng.choice('abc') for _ in range(block_len))
    reps = max(2, (max(1, size) // block_len))
    s = (block * reps)[:max(block_len, min(10000, max(1, size)))]
    return [s]
stress.note: guarantees many true cases while truncation occasionally breaks periodicity

stress.description: random strings over a tiny alphabet so accidental periodicity is rare but possible
stress.note: mostly false cases to balance the periodic generator

complexity.time: O(n^2) worst case for the search
